title: Four New Chameleon Species Found in Tropical “Sky Islands”
gist: A Vertebrate Zoology study of northern Mozambique's isolated mountaintop 'sky island' forests identifies six Nadzikambia chameleon species — four new to science (two named for Jane Goodall and Rosalind Franklin) — even as logging degrades the misty habitats that make them endemic.

insights.claim: Comparing DNA and body features of 31 chameleons across six Mozambican sky islands, herpetologists determined they represent six distinct Nadzikambia species, four of which are new to science.
insights.speaker: Devin Reese
insights.so_what: Genomics-plus-morphology keeps uncovering hidden endemic diversity in understudied regions; basic biodiversity science.
insights.quote: the chameleons represented six distinct species, four of which are new to science
insights.locator: Based on the combination of genomes and morphology

insights.claim: Two new chameleon species were named for primate biologist Jane Goodall (N. goodallae) and DNA chemist Rosalind Franklin (N. franklinae).
insights.speaker: Devin Reese
insights.so_what: Naming convention honors pioneering female scientists; cultural note only.
insights.quote: named in honor of primate biologist Jane Goodall (N. goodallae) and DNA chemist Rosalind Franklin (N. franklinae)
insights.locator: Two of the chameleon species new to science

insights.claim: Mountaintop rainforests rising sharply from dry savanna trap clouds and rain to create cool, moist refuges, isolating each forest as a 'sky island' whose species occur nowhere else on Earth.
insights.speaker: Devin Reese
insights.so_what: A clean model of geographic isolation driving speciation and endemism.
insights.quote: These mountains rise sharply from the surrounding savanna, trapping clouds and rain and creating cool, moist refuges in an otherwise dry landscape

insights.claim: With sky islands separated by at least 28 miles of unsuitable savanna habitat, forest-bound chameleons show little or no movement between them, reinforcing isolation over millions of years.
insights.speaker: Devin Reese
insights.so_what: Quantifies the dispersal barrier underlying the endemism.
insights.quote: it’s no wonder that there’s been little or no movement of chameleons between them
insights.locator: With the sky islands surveyed in this study separated

insights.claim: Many sky island forests are degrading from logging — on Mulanje's slopes the endemic chameleon N. mlanjensis has been deemed endangered — meaning species face extinction even as they are just being discovered.
insights.speaker: Devin Reese
insights.so_what: Habitat loss is outpacing discovery; relevant to biodiversity-conservation and habitat-protection priorities.
insights.quote: many of the sky island forests are degrading from logging activities
insights.locator: Unfortunately, many of the sky island forests
